titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Jonathan Brace Papers
titlestmt.author: Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 16110680.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday February 2, 2022 at 04:11

did.unittitle: Jonathan Brace Papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Letters, journals, and sermons by or relating to Jonathan Brace, Congregationalist minister at First Congregational Church in Litchfield and Milford, Connecticut, 1785-1877. Some materials pertain to members of the Brace family, specifically Jonathan Brace's grandfather, Congressman Jonathan Brace (1754-1837).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Correspondence, 1785-1877. II. Papers, 1808-1876.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Jonathan Brace (1810-1877) graduated from Amherst College in 1831 and pursued theological studies in Andover, Massachusetts; New Haven, Connecticut; and Princeton, New Jersey. In 1844, after having served as pastor of the Congregational Church in Litchfield, Connecticut, for about eight years, he resigned from his post. By the following year, he had resumed his ministry at the First Congregational Church in Milford, Connecticut.
Brace was married to Sarah E. Brace ( -1866), with whom he had a son, William Henry Brace ( -1846), and daughter, Lucy Brace.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jonathan Brace Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters, journals, and sermons by or relating to Jonathan Brace, Congregationalist minister at First Congregational Church in Litchfield and Milford, Connecticut, 1785-1877. Some materials pertain to members of the Brace family, specifically Jonathan Brace's grandfather, Congressman Jonathan Brace (1754-1837).

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Jonathan Brace Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Correspondence

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged alphabetically by correspondent.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains letters to or from Jonathan Brace; his grandfather, Congressman Jonathan Brace (1754-1837); and other members of the Brace family.

did.unittitle: Atwater, Lyman H. - 2 autograph letters, signed, to Jonathan Brace
Averill(?), Roget - autograph letter, signed, to Dr. Rev. Jonathan Brace
Beach, Annie - 5 autograph letters, signed, to Lucy Brace
Brace, E. - autograph letter, signed, to daughter, Sarah Brace
Brace, Franny - 9 autograph letters, signed, to father, Hon. Jonathan Brace
Brace, (F. A.?) - autograph letter, signed, to brother, Rev. Jonathan Brace
Brace, (J. M.?) - autograph letter, signed, to Jonathan Brace
Brace, Hon. Jonathan - autograph letter, signed, to wife, Anna Brace
Brace, Hon. Jonathan - 7 autograph letters, signed, to Franny Brace
Brace, Hon. Jonathan - 4 autograph letters, signed to grandson, Jonathan Brace
Brace, Hon. Jonathan - 2 autograph letters, signed, to son, Thomas K. Brace

Brace, Hon. Jonathan - 2 autograph letters, signed, to Professor Hall
Brace, Hon. Jonathan - 3 autograph letters, signed, to Benjamin Tallmadge
Brace, Hon. Jonathan - 1 manuscript, signed, and 2 autograph letters, signed, to Governor Trumbull
Brace, Rev. Jonathan - 8 autograph letters, signed, to his parents, Thomas K. and Lucy Brace

Brace, Rev. Jonathan - autograph letter, signed, to grandfather, Hon. Jonathan Brace
Brace, Rev. Jonathan - 2 autograph letters, signed, to daughter, Lucy Brace
Brace, Lucy Lee - 9 autograph letters, signed, to parents, Rev. Jonathan and Sarah Brace
Brace, Samuel W. - autograph letter, signed, to Rev. Dr. Jonathan Brace
Brace, Sarah E. - 5 autograph letters, signed, to daughter, Lucy Brace
Brace, Thomas K. - autograph letter, signed, to father, Hon. Jonathan Brace
Brace, Thomas K. - 2 autograph letters, signed, to son, Rev. Jonathan Brace
Buckingham, Sarah A. - autograph letter, signed, to "Mrs. Brace"
Bullock, A. H. - autograph letter, signed,to "Mr. Brace"
Chittenden, R. H. - autograph letter, signed, to Rev. Jonathan Brace
Chittenden, Thomas - autograph letter, signed, to Hon. Jonathan Brace
[Fay?], Lusie M. - autograph letter, signed, to Lucy Brace
Goodell, C. L. - 2 autograph letters, signed, to Rev. Jonathan Brace
Goodrich, Chancey - 3 autograph letters, signed, to Hon. Jonathan Brace
Granger, Gideon - autograph letter, signed, to Hon. Jonathan Brace
Hall, Professor F. - autograph letter, signed, to Hon. Jonathan Brace, with a copy of Brace's reply
Hamilton Literary Association - Minutes and autograph letter, signed, to Thomas K. Brace
Hartford, Common Council of the City of - Acceptance of Thomas K. Brace's resignation from the Office of Mayor
Hawes, J. - autograph letter, signed, to Rev. Jonathan Brace, with a copy of Brace's reply
Henry, James W. - autograph letter, signed, to Hon. Jonathan Brace
Hulhall(?), Frances L. - autograph letter, signed, to Lucy Brace
Kellogg, Martha L. - autograph letter, signed, to Rev. Jonathan Brace
Kimberly, Anson - autograph letter, signed, to Hon. Jonathan Brace
Litchfield, First Congregational Society of - Minutes and Resolutions
Loomis, S. L. - 2 autograph letters, signed, to Thomas K. Brace
Magill, S. H. - autograph letter, signed, to Rev. Jonathan Brace
Milford, First Church and Society of - autograph letter, signed, to Rev. Jonathan Brace
Milford, First Ecclesiastical Society of - Preamble and Resolutions
Miller, Samuel - Verification of enrollment of Rev. Jonathan Brace at Princeton Theological Seminary
New Britain, South Church and Congregation of - autograph letter, signed, to Rev. Jonathan Brace
New Haven, Association of the Eastern District of - autograph letter, signed, to Rev. Jonathan Brace
Peck, Charles - autograph letter, signed, to Rev. Jonathan Brace
Putnam, Austin - autograph letter, signed, to Rev. Jonathan Brace
Sabin, Lewis - 4 autograph letters, signed, to Rev. Jonathan Brace
Shepard, H.(?) - autograph letter, signed, to Rev. Jonathan Brace
Smith, Andrew A. - Address concerning the death of William H. Brace (to the Hamilton Literary Association?)
Smith, F. H. - autograph letter, signed, to Rev. Jonathan Brace
Sterling, Emilie E. - autograph letter, signed, to Lucy Brace
Sulliven(?), J. P. - autograph letter, signed, to Rev. Jonathan Brace, with letters from William Moore
Tallmadge, Benjamin - 6 autograph letters, signed, to Hon. Jonathan Brace
Tompkins, Daniel D. - 2 autograph letters, signed, to Hon. Jonathan Brace
Treadwell, John - 2 autograph letters, signed, to Hon. Jonathan Brace
Trumbull, Jonathan - 2 autograph letters, signed, to Hon. Jonathan Brace
Western Reserve Historical Society - autograph letter, signed, to Rev. Jonathan Brace
Wheaton, N. S. - autograph letter, signed, to Hon. Jonathan Brace
Papers

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is arranged roughly by format.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains circa 30 sermons, journals, and miscellaneous manuscripts of Jonathan Brace.

did.unittitle: Typescript sermons
Manuscript sermons
Manuscript journal
Manuscript journal
Manuscript notebook with newspaper clippings, titled "For My Children"
Brace, Rev. Jonathan - Manuscript account book

Manuscript account book of charitable contributions
Manuscript account book
Manuscript funeral sketch book
Manuscript journal - "List of Sermons and Dates Preached"
Manuscript notebook listing five hundred sermons and their texts
Manuscript marriage account book
Manuscript journal - "List of Sermons and Dates Preached"
Manuscript account book
Miscellaneous Brace family papers
